findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the `code-review: session:{id}` local title scheme is implemented in the code-review sidecar/runner before ADR 0037 documents it as an implemented, enforced keyspace disjoint from the Gerrit scheme.
findings.finding: The plan's session keyspace (`session:<id>` / `code-review: session:{id}` title scheme for local reviews) is described as implemented and disjoint from the Gerrit change keyspace, with `latest_code_review_result` in sidecar.py matching only its own title scheme. No implementation of the `code-review: session:{id}` title format exists anywhere in the codebase (only the Gerrit `code-review: {change_id} @ {revision}` is present in voter.py). The disjointness invariant — that the reader only matches its own scheme — cannot be verified without the local-session title emission code.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section bullet 5 (typed session/change keyspaces); AC4 (code-review gate doc must document keying)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a definition/description of 'carryover labeling' to the What section (what it is, how it works, which module implements it) so AC4's gate-doc requirement is fully specified.
findings.finding: The term 'carryover labeling' appears in AC4 and in the 'Also update the docs' sentence but is never defined or described anywhere in the plan body. An implementer updating the code-review gate doc to cover 'carryover labeling' has no specification of what that label mechanism is, how it works, or where it is implemented.
findings.location: AC4 and 'Also update the docs to cross-link' paragraph in the What section
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add a bullet or inline note such as: 'Carryover labeling — a finding that has been dropped by the region-gated floor is labeled (e.g. tagged advisory/suppressed) to indicate it was seen and dropped, implemented in [module]; document this behavior in the gate doc so readers understand dropped findings are not silently lost.'

findings.suggested_fix: Add the concrete file path to Scope: e.g., `docs/code-review-gate.md` (NEW or EXISTING — clarify). Update AC4 to read: 'The code-review gate doc (`docs/code-review-gate.md`) documents the novelty convergence behavior…'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before writing the ADR, confirm that the cited implementation modules and function names (region_gate.py, score_code_novelty, apply_region_gated_floor, latest_code_review_result, code_review_novelty.md, 'code-novelty' reviewer id, code_review_novelty contract) match what is actually present in the feature branch (c639/3bff/e51b/c3e4) — or reframe the ADR status as 'Proposed' rather than 'Accepted/Implemented' if those commits have not yet merged to the branch the ADR is written against.
findings.finding: The plan's AC1 promises the ADR cites 'its backing implementation module so a reader can verify it,' but several core modules and functions referenced do not exist in the codebase (region_gate.py, score_code_novelty, apply_region_gated_floor, latest_code_review_result, code_review_novelty.md, the code_review_novelty contract, and the 'code-novelty' reviewer id are all absent). If the ADR is written with these citations and the implementation lands under different module paths or function names, the ADR's verifiability promise will be false from day one.
findings.location: What section / AC1 / Scope ('Documents the IMPLEMENTED behavior landed by c639 / 3bff / e51b + the c3e4 surfaced-only fix')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a pre-writing step: enumerate the actual module paths and function names from the epic's landed commits before drafting the ADR, and cross-check each citation in the What section against the live code. Alternatively, mark the ADR status as 'Proposed' with a note that it targets the epic's feature stack pending merge, so readers know the citations are forward-looking.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before writing ADR 0037 with status 'Accepted,' confirm that region_gate.py (REGION_UNCHANGED/CHANGED/UNKNOWN), `score_code_novelty`/`apply_region_gated_floor` in workflow_ops.py, `latest_code_review_result`/`reviewed_file_hashes` in sidecar.py, and the `code_review_novelty.md` prompt + `code-novelty` reviewer id are all present in the repo — or change ADR status to 'Proposed' until the implementation stack lands.
findings.finding: The plan's 'What' section and every AC cite `src/rebar/llm/code_review/region_gate.py` (REGION_UNCHANGED/REGION_CHANGED/REGION_UNKNOWN constants + detector), `score_code_novelty` and `apply_region_gated_floor` in `workflow_ops.py`, `latest_code_review_result` in `sidecar.py`, `reviewed_file_hashes` in `sidecar.py`, and `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code_review_novelty.md` (plus the `code-novelty` reviewer id) as IMPLEMENTED modules the ADR will cite for verifiability. Tool search finds none of these exist in the repo. The plan's declared status ('Accepted — documents DECISIONS that are IMPLEMENTED') and its explicit instruction that 'each element [cites] its backing implementation module so a reader can verify it' are unfulfillable if the modules don't exist when the ADR is written.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section — bullet 1 (score_code_novelty / apply_region_gated_floor), bullet 2 (region_gate.py + tri-state), bullet 3 (latest_code_review_result), bullet 6 (reviewed_file_hashes); AC1; also src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py, sidecar.py, region_gate.py, reviewers/code_review_novelty.md
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) land the c639/3bff/e51b/c3e4 implementation stack before or concurrently with this ADR task so every cited module is greppable, or (b) change the ADR status to 'Proposed' and note which children are pending, converting each implementation-module citation to a forward reference ('will live at …') until the stack lands.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the ADR slot collision: `0037` is already doubly occupied (`0037-oss-contribution-intake-posture.md`, `0037-reconciler-live-validation-harness.md`) and `0038` is also taken (`0038-governance-artifacts.md`); update the plan's target filename to the actual next free slot number before implementation.
findings.finding: The plan hard-codes the ADR file path as `docs/adr/0037-code-review-novelty-convergence.md`, but the `0037` slot is already doubly occupied in the repository: `0037-oss-contribution-intake-posture.md` and `0037-reconciler-live-validation-harness.md` both exist. This means the plan's target file path collides with prior art in the ADR namespace, and the next available slot (0038) is also taken by `0038-governance-artifacts.md`.
findings.location: ## What — `docs/adr/0037-code-review-novelty-convergence.md` (NEW); ## Scope
findings.suggested_fix: Scan `docs/adr/` for the highest existing slot number and assign `0039-code-review-novelty-convergence.md` (or whatever the next free slot is). Update all references to the filename in the plan's What, Scope, and AC sections.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: Destructive/irreversible operation(s) with no stated safeguard.
findings.suggested_fix: State the safeguard (backup, dry-run, reversible migration, rollback) or justify the irreversibility explicitly (T4).

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least a lightweight proving command per AC (e.g., `grep -q 'region_gated_floor' docs/adr/0037-code-review-novelty-convergence.md` for AC1, `grep -q 'dso-style arbiter' docs/adr/0037-code-review-novelty-convergence.md` for AC2, and a link-check for AC5) so completion is machine-verifiable.
findings.finding: None of the five acceptance criteria name a concrete proving command (e.g., grep, file-existence check, link validator) that produces machine-readable evidence of completion; all ACs rely solely on implicit human inspection of the produced documents.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria section (all five ACs)
findings.suggested_fix:
Append a 'Verification' subsection to the Acceptance Criteria with grep anchors, e.g.:
```
grep -q 'region_gated_floor' docs/adr/0037-code-review-novelty-convergence.md
grep -q 'dso-style arbiter' docs/adr/0037-code-review-novelty-convergence.md
grep -q 'permanent' docs/adr/0037-code-review-novelty-convergence.md
grep -q 'region_gated_floor' docs/reuse-surface.md
```
For AC4, add a grep on the named gate doc path once it is specified.
